calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Dec-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

THERESA BICHSEL, Esq., of the STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Department of Industrial Relations, 415.703.4863, for the California Labor Commission.

STEVEN WONG, Esq., of FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP, 415.364.5540, for Defendant.

RE: PLAINTIFF ISAAC GERONA's Motion For Order Determining Claim Of Exemption.

Ruling is ARGUED. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The claim of exemption is denied. Judgment Debtor bears the burden of showing the property is exempt from collection. (Civ. Proc. Code, Sec. 703.580(b).) Judgment Debtor's claim does not accurately reflect her assets, omitting, for example, her beneficial interest in real property equity. (Bichsel Decl., Ex. 6, 8 & 16.) Judgment Debtor's claim of mistake is not creditable. Nor does the court credit Judgment Debtor's representations regarding the identified assets or their nature. For example, she does not trace her social security payments to her Chase account holdings.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: PLAINTIFF ISAAC GERONA HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. Matter set to be heard on December 15, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)
